intro: Cervaria alsatica, also known as Alpine pennycress, is a perennial flowering plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Cervaria alsatica include glucosinolates, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cervaria alsatica is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory infections.
